skill.name: agent-evaluation
skill.description: Helps teams design practical evaluations for LLM agents, including behavioral tests, capability checks, reliability metrics, and production monitoring.
skill.summary: Design repeatable tests and metrics for evaluating LLM agent quality.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against the only scanned file, SKILL.md. The external command alerts are Markdown backticks around related skill names, and the weak crypto alerts are keyword false positives in descriptive text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 64 uses backticks to format related skill names. It does not instruct the agent to execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full line is documentation text listing related skills. There is no command, shell syntax, or execution instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: SKILL.md lines 3 and 21 contain descriptive text and a capability label. No cryptographic algorithm or implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural language metadata and a bullet label. No code, crypto API, key handling, or hashing operation appears.

content.user_title: Evaluate AI Agents With Reliable Tests
content.value_statement: Agent behavior can vary across runs, models, and real production tasks. This skill helps teams create practical tests, metrics, and monitoring plans.
actual_capabilities: Designs behavioral tests for LLM agent workflows.
Defines capability assessments for specific agent tasks.
Plans repeated-run testing for variable agent outputs.
Identifies evaluation metrics for reliability and regressions.
Highlights risks from benchmark overfitting and data leakage.
limitations: Does not run automated tests by itself.
Does not provide a benchmark dataset.
Requires domain examples to create strong evaluations.

use_cases.title: Pre-Release Agent Testing
use_cases.description: Create evaluation plans before launching a customer-facing agent.

use_cases.title: Regression Evaluation
use_cases.description: Compare agent behavior across prompt, model, or tool changes.

use_cases.title: Capability Benchmarking
use_cases.description: Measure how well an agent completes important task categories.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Core Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate my agent for a support workflow. Identify key behaviors, likely failure modes, and three practical tests.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Regression Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing a prompt or model change
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a regression test plan for this agent change. Include repeated runs, pass criteria, and review steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design Capability Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Design capability metrics for an agent that completes research tasks. Include task categories, scoring rules, and reliability checks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this agent for production readiness. Assess benchmark gaps, adversarial risks, monitoring needs, and data leakage controls.

output_examples.input: A support agent needs evaluation before release.
output_examples.output: A useful result would list core support behaviors, unstable response areas, repeated-run tests, escalation checks, and release thresholds.

output_examples.input: A team changed the model behind an existing agent.
output_examples.output: A useful result would compare old and new behavior, define regression checks, and recommend monitoring for production drift.

output_examples.input: An agent scores well on benchmarks but fails customer tasks.
output_examples.output: A useful result would identify benchmark gaps, propose task-realistic tests, and add metrics tied to customer outcomes.

best_practices: Run tests multiple times to measure behavior variance.
Use real task examples and separate holdout data.
Track production outcomes alongside benchmark scores.
anti_patterns: Relying on one successful test run.
Scoring exact wording instead of task success.

faq.question: What does this skill help evaluate?
faq.answer: It helps evaluate LLM agents, including behavior, capabilities, reliability, regressions, and production readiness.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It helps design evaluation plans, metrics, and review criteria.

faq.question: Can it compare two agent versions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline regression tests and comparison criteria for prompt, model, or tool changes.

faq.question: Does it include benchmark datasets?
faq.answer: No. You need to provide task examples, expected outcomes, or evaluation data.

faq.question: Is it useful for production monitoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It can recommend reliability metrics, drift checks, and review signals.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
